qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebSocket permessage-deflate decompression amplification enables CPU denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets WebSocket permessage-deflate decompression in PerMessageDeflate.java, while the advisory describes a padding oracle in Apache Tomcat Tribes EncryptInterceptor.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described EncryptInterceptor padding oracle or an equivalent vulnerable source-to-sink/control-flow condition. The only final qualifying finding is an unrelated WebSocket permessage-deflate decompression amplification denial-of-service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-h468-7pvh-8vr8 describes a padding oracle vulnerability in Apache Tomcat's EncryptInterceptor with default configuration, affecting org.apache.tomcat:tomcat-tribes and org.apache.tomcat:tomcat.
The qualifying finding websocket-permessage-deflate-dos cites PerMessageDeflate.java, WsFrameBase.java, and websocket Constants.java as its vulnerable code path.
The qualifying finding's reported impact is CPU denial-of-service from WebSocket decompression amplification, not confidentiality loss from a cryptographic padding oracle.
